message:
fix(abi,rcu,printf): harden alignment checks, fix probe chain corruption, add %a format

malloc_abi: memalign() now validates that alignment is a power of two before
proceeding, matching POSIX requirements. aligned_alloc() adds the same
is_power_of_two() guard alongside the existing size-multiple-of-alignment
check, per C11 semantics. Previously, non-power-of-two alignments could
produce subtly misaligned allocations.

string_abi: strncmp() and strncasecmp() now properly bound the comparison
limit when only one of the two operand bounds is known (Some/None arms),
instead of falling through to the unbounded `n`. This prevents potential
over-reads when one string is near a page boundary. strcasecmp() and strsep()
now condition the +1 slice extension on whether scan_c_string actually found
a NUL terminator, avoiding a 1-byte over-read on unterminated buffers.

rcu: Introduces tombstone-based slot management for the RCU reader table.
Previously, rcu_unregister_thread() reset slots to SLOT_EMPTY, which broke
linear probe chains and caused downstream registrations to silently fail
lookups for threads that hashed past the now-empty gap. Unregistration now
writes SLOT_TOMBSTONE (u32::MAX), and registration probes continue past
tombstones while preferring to reclaim them for new registrations.

The ReaderSlot struct is corrected to #[repr(C, align(64))] with proper
padding accounting for the 4-byte alignment gap between tid (u32) and
epoch (u64), fixing a 4-byte padding miscalculation (was 52, now 48).

process_rcu_callbacks() gains an AtomicBool guard to prevent concurrent
callback processing, which could cause double-invocation of freed callbacks.

printf: Adds %a/%A hexadecimal floating-point formatting. Fixes %e/%E
precision-zero alt_form output (was missing the trailing dot). Corrects
log10 imprecision for mantissa normalization by adding post-hoc correction
for values that round to >=10 or <1 after initial exponent computation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
